Department of Human Services Year in Review 2025
Community Visioning Effort Launches for Penn State New Kensington Campus
A new public engagement initiative invites residents, businesses, alumni and community leaders to help shape a shared vision for the future of the Penn State New Kensington campus through surveys and community events this summer.
Read on...
Act 88 Notice
In accordance with the reporting requirements of Act 88 of 2022, Westmoreland County received a total of 20954 mail-in and absentee ballots by 8pm on the day of the May 19, 2026 General Primary Election.
2026 Westmoreland County Adopted Budget
Click here to view.
ATTENTION VETERANS: Scam Alert
Read on...
UPDATE! Passport Service Hours Change
Beginning on January 1, 2026, we will again be accepting appointments for Thursday after hours Passport Services!
Act 88 Notice
In accordance with Act 88’s reporting requirements, Westmoreland County received an unofficial total of 17,989 mail-in and absentee ballots by 8pm on Election Day for the May 20, 2025 Municipality Primaries.
Chelsea Building Products, ARCO break ground on Commerce Crossing project
Chelsea Building Products and ARCO Pittsburgh broke ground on a project to build a 228,000-square-foot manufacturing facility at Commerce Crossing at Westmoreland yesterday morning, marking a major investment in Westmoreland County’s industrial growth.
Read on...
Westmoreland County Announces BEAD Guideline Policy
Westmoreland County announces guidelines for internet service providers (ISPs) around its commitment to financially support Pennsylvania Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment Program (BEAD) applications within the County.
Read on...
Westmoreland Manor Respite Program
Need a break? Come to Westmoreland Manor!
Read on...
DA unveils, dedicates Victim Witness Waiting Room designed by Fred Rogers Institute
Westmoreland County District Attorney Nicole W. Ziccarelli is honored to unveil and dedicate the new Victim Witness Waiting Room at the District Attorney’s Office.
Read on...
Westmoreland County Partners with Turtle Creek Watershed Association
Westmoreland County announced a partnership with the Turtle Creek Watershed Association to begin cleanup of the Irwin (mine) Discharge. The project is the first step in addressing the largest untreated abandoned mine discharge in Western Pennsylvania.
Read on...
Westmoreland County Enhances Online Presence
As of May 15, 2024, Westmoreland County's online presence has moved to www.westmorelandcountypa.gov
Read on...
Feedback from Young Adults with Disabilities
Westmoreland County Department of Human Services wants young adults with disabilities to meet their own goals and enjoy a good life.  Please take our survey!
Read on...

Reporting Period: Date of Award – June 30, 2022
